info.title: Azure File Storage
info.description: Azure File Storage provides scalable file shares in the cloud using SMB and NFS protocols.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL of the service account, share, directory or file that is the target of the desired operation.

get.description: Reads or downloads a file from the system, including its metadata and properties.

parameters.description: Specifies the version of the operation to use for this request.

parameters.name: x-ms-client-request-id
parameters.description: An opaque, globally-unique, client-generated string identifier for the request.

parameters.description: The name of the target share.

parameters.description: The path of the target directory.

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: The path of the target file.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: The timeout parameter is expressed in seconds.

parameters.name: x-ms-range
parameters.description: Return file data only from the specified byte range.

parameters.name: x-ms-range-get-content-md5
parameters.description: When this header is set to true and specified together with the Range header, the service returns the MD5 hash for the range, as long as the range is less than or equal to 4 MB in size.

parameters.name: x-ms-lease-id
parameters.description: If specified, the lease ID must match the lease ID of the file.

parameters.description: The name of the target share.

parameters.description: The path of the target directory.

parameters.description: The path of the target file.

parameters.name: x-ms-content-length
parameters.description: Specifies the number of bytes being transmitted in the request body. When the x-ms-write header is set to clear, the value of this header must be set to zero.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: The timeout parameter is expressed in seconds.

parameters.name: x-ms-type
parameters.description: Dummy constant parameter, file type can only be file.

parameters.name: x-ms-content-type
parameters.description: Sets the MIME content type of the file. The default type is 'application/octet-stream'.

parameters.name: x-ms-content-encoding
parameters.description: Specifies which content encodings have been applied to the file.

parameters.name: x-ms-content-language
parameters.description: Specifies the natural languages used by this resource.

parameters.name: x-ms-cache-control
parameters.description: Sets the file's cache control. The File service stores this value but does not use or modify it.

parameters.name: x-ms-content-md5
parameters.description: An MD5 hash of the file content. This hash is used to verify the integrity of the file during transport.

parameters.name: x-ms-content-disposition
parameters.description: Sets the file's Content-Disposition header.

parameters.name: x-ms-meta
parameters.description: Optional. User-defined metadata for the resource.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-permission
parameters.description: If specified the permission (security descriptor) shall be set for the directory/file. This header can be used if Permission size is <= 8KB, else x-ms-file-permission-key header shall be used. Default value: Inherit. If SDDL is specified as input, it must have owner, group and dacl. Note: Only one of the x-ms-file-permission or x-ms-file-permission-key should be specified.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-permission-key
parameters.description: Key of the permission to be set for the directory/file. Note: Only one of the x-ms-file-permission or x-ms-file-permission-key should be specified.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-attributes
parameters.description: If specified, the provided file attributes shall be set. Default value: 'Archive' for file and 'Directory' for directory. 'None' can also be specified as default.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-creation-time
parameters.description: Creation time for the file/directory. Default value: Now.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-last-write-time
parameters.description: Last write time for the file/directory. Default value: Now.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-change-time
parameters.description: Change time for the file/directory. Default value: Now.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-permission-format
parameters.description: Optional. Used to set permission format.

parameters.name: x-ms-lease-id
parameters.description: If specified, the lease ID must match the lease ID of the file.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-request-intent
parameters.description: Valid values are 'backup'.

parameters.name: x-ms-owner
parameters.description: Optional, NFS only. The owner of the file or directory.

parameters.name: x-ms-group
parameters.description: Optional, NFS only. The owning group of the file or directory.

parameters.name: x-ms-mode
parameters.description: Optional, NFS only. The file mode of the file or directory.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-file-type
parameters.description: Optional, NFS only. Type of the file or directory.

parameters.name: Content-MD5
parameters.description: An MD5 hash of the content. This hash is used to verify the integrity of the data during transport.

parameters.name: x-ms-file-property-semantics
parameters.description: SMB only. Default value is New.

parameters.name: Content-Length
parameters.description: Optional. Specifies the content length of the file.

parameters.name: x-ms-lease-break-period
parameters.description: For a break operation, proposed duration the lease should continue before it is broken, in seconds, between 0 and 60. This break period is only used if it is shorter than the time remaining on the lease. If longer, the time remaining on the lease is used. A new lease will not be available before the break period has expired, but the lease may be held for longer than the break period. If this header does not appear with a break operation, a fixed-duration lease breaks after the remaining lease period elapses, and an infinite lease breaks immediately.

CorsRule.description:
CORS is an HTTP feature that enables a web application running under one domain
to access resources in another domain. Web browsers implement a security
restriction known as same-origin policy that prevents a web page from calling
APIs in a different domain; CORS provides a secure way to allow one domain (the
origin domain) to call APIs in another domain.

AllowedOrigins.description:
The origin domains that are permitted to make a request against the storage
service via CORS. The origin domain is the domain from which the request
originates. Note that the origin must be an exact case-sensitive match with the
origin that the user age sends to the service. You can also use the wildcard
character '*' to allow all origin domains to make requests via CORS.

AllowedMethods.description:
The methods (HTTP request verbs) that the origin domain may use for a CORS
request. (comma separated)

AllowedHeaders.description: The request headers that the origin domain may specify on the CORS request.

ExposedHeaders.description:
The response headers that may be sent in the response to the CORS request and
exposed by the browser to the request issuer.

MaxAgeInSeconds.description:
The maximum amount time that a browser should cache the preflight OPTIONS
request.

Content-Length.description:
Content length of the file. This value may not be up-to-date since an SMB
client may have modified the file locally. The value of Content-Length may not
reflect that fact until the handle is closed or the op-lock is broken. To
retrieve current property values, call Get File Properties.
